summary: Set 084 Session 2 (F3 + the close backstop). F3 -- only stamped evidence corroborates: new ai_router/verification_stamp.py defines the additive stamp fields (STAMP_FIELDS is the authoritative list: source, evidence_sha256, template_id + pinned normalized template_sha256, verifier_model, orchestrator_effective_provider, artifact_path + byte-exact artifact_sha256, package_version, evidence_base, work_diff_sha256, verdict; all null on historical rows). The template id is minted in code (session-verification-v1) so the canonical adversarial template stays byte-identical (L-069-2) and an operator template change is an explicit version bump, never an accidental pass. record_call writes the stamp; route() completes it at record time (verifier model post-escalation; artifact hash over the exact bytes the producer writes with newline='') on BOTH the api and copilot-cli paths, and refuses a stamp outside task_type=session-verification; verify_session builds and passes the stamp. The Set 083 close gate's api arm now accepts ONLY rows with a valid, internally consistent stamp (shared find_session_verification_evidence + validate_stamped_row -- one path for the gate and the backstop, L-069-1): template mismatch, missing fields, edited artifacts, copied stamps, and same-provider verifiers all fail closed with named reasons; a bare route() row (the incident-3 shape) no longer corroborates; refusals keep naming the venv-qualified sanctioned command. The stamp is documented as drift/affordance control, not cryptography. THE CLOSE BACKSTOP (structural): new ai_router/close_backstop.py wired into close_session.run before the gate chain -- on a Full-tier close without settling stamped evidence, the framework runs the verification itself in-process through the S1 F1/F2 machinery (evidence assembled against the last commit before the session's startedAt, canonical template, registry-resolved provider exclusion, stamped row, raw artifacts, disposition patch), then proceeds on VERIFIED / Minor-only, refuses with findings on blocking ISSUES_FOUND, and blocks explicitly on verification_unavailable or double transport failure -- never a pass. Respects budget.yaml (zero-budget passthrough untouched), the two-attempt ladder, and the close lock (idempotent); verify_session pre-empts it; --manual-verify remains the attested bypass; --force gets no special treatment -- an unverified force-close receives the same in-process verification (I-084-S2-1) -- and backstop-written artifacts are close-out bookkeeping the working-tree gate tolerates for that one close (the session-events.jsonl precedent). Layer-1 matrix: all three live incidents as end-to-end regressions in test_close_backstop.py, producer-side stamp unit tests incl. the pinned-hash tripwire, stamp-layer gate tests; existing close-path fixtures upgraded to stamped evidence via shared tests/stamp_fixtures.py; a conftest autouse guard refuses live routed calls from the backstop under pytest. Fixed a live I-084-S1-2-class defect found by the new tests: the backstop catches VerificationUnavailableError under both module identities. Dogfood (spec step 2.5), described within its evidence (L-073-1): no manual verify_session was ever run this session; the close itself invoked the backstop LIVE -- round 1 (gpt-5-4, anthropic excluded by resolution, $0.25) returned 3 Major findings and REFUSED the close, the live proof that the framework holds the last word. All three findings were accepted and remediated (ledger I-084-S2-1: --force now runs the backstop too; I-084-S2-2: TEMPLATE_HASHES pins each template id to an immutable normalized hash, enforced producer- and consumer-side, with a tripwire test; I-084-S2-3: this narrative replaced the earlier ahead-of-evidence dogfood claim). Round 2 (live, $0.35) refused again with two findings that exposed a genuine omission the fix closes as a class (I-084-S2-4): the backstop sent NO up-front conventions block, violating the promoted L-064-10 Convention -- so a mid-close verifier structurally sees an 'unfinished close' (the evidence can never contain closeout_succeeded during the verification that gates it) and cannot know the severity-derived blocking discipline this set's spec non-goals preserve (L-071-1; Minor-only proceeds -- the round-2 'ISSUES_FOUND must always refuse' reading is the bare-token fallacy the discipline exists to correct). The backstop now builds its own conventions preamble (mid-close view; L-071-1 predicate; narrow re-verify rounds) in the Original Task slot -- the canonical template untouched, the F3 binding intact. Round 3 (live, $0.42) found two NEW genuine correctness defects, both accepted and fixed: I-084-S2-5 stale evidence could settle a later close -- the stamp now binds to the repo state via two additional fields (evidence_base, the resolved diff-base sha; work_diff_sha256, a canonical-exclusion work-diff hash the close gate RECOMPUTES against the tree being closed -- post-verification work changes make the row stale and the backstop runs a fresh round); I-084-S2-6 the fresh-repo / missing-startedAt fallback silently degraded the evidence to a HEAD diff -- no pre-session commit now diffs from git's empty tree (the session's work IS the whole tree) and a missing startedAt fails closed naming verify_session --diff-base. Round 4 (live, $0.50) found two more genuine Majors, both accepted and fixed: I-084-S2-7 the claimed verdict was never bound to the stamped evidence (a hand-flipped disposition could ride a refusing row) -- the stamp now carries the VERDICT, parsed at record time from the same bytes artifact_sha256 binds, and both consumers (the close gate and the backstop's skip predicate) require claim == stamped verdict; I-084-S2-5 (round-4 refinement, previously incomplete) the freshness exclusions were too broad (whole set dir + docs/planning) -- narrowed to the exact close-out bookkeeping files (artifacts, envelopes, disposition, ledgers, state, change-log, lock files), and the freshness primitive became a content digest (path + blob hash of everything differing from base, tracked or untracked) so an untracked deliverable surviving the sanctioned commit does not false-stale while any content change does. Both round-4 nits fixed (docstring flag interplay; hardcoded field counts). Round 5 (live, $0.39) converged to one Major, accepted and fixed (I-084-S2-8, the round-4 binding's remaining gap): when multiple valid stamped rows disagree, claim-in-set membership allowed cherry-picking an older favorable row past a later refusing verification -- the LATEST valid stamped row is now the one authoritative result in both consumers, and the backstop's Minor-only settlement reads the findings envelope PAIRED to that row's own round (derived from its artifact path), never a global latest. Round 6 (live, $0.50) found one Major, accepted and fixed (I-084-S2-9): backstop bookkeeping was tolerated only on the run that wrote it, so a backstop-VERIFIED run whose close failed on a LATER gate left a rerun tripping working_tree_clean on the prior round's uncommitted artifacts -- the evidence-present skip now rediscovers the settling row's bookkeeping paths (artifact, paired findings envelope, patched disposition) and keeps passing them to the working-tree gate; regression test covers backstop-VERIFIED -> later-gate failure -> idempotent rerun (also closing the round-6 nit about the untested skip path). Round 7 (live, $0.41) found one Major, accepted and fixed (I-084-S2-10): the row-level verdict field was trusted on read -- validate_stamped_row now RE-DERIVES the verdict from the hash-validated artifact bytes and refuses a row whose verdict field does not match (a forged row-level flip over an unchanged ISSUES_FOUND artifact fails closed; regression test added). Round-7 nits fixed: superseded ledger wording corrected by appended note (activity-log entries are append-only); the last hardcoded field-count echoes replaced by pointers to STAMP_FIELDS. Round 8 (live, $0.42) found two Majors, both accepted and fixed: I-084-S2-11 the drifted-template ValueError unwound as a traceback -- verify_session and the backstop now convert it to a controlled fail-closed outcome with remediation (exit 3 / gate_failed naming the version bump; no metered call; integration regressions on both paths); I-084-S2-12 the round>1 conventions text asserted 'earlier findings were remediated' unconditionally -- reworded to facts the backstop actually knows (round history is on disk; judge the CURRENT state; re-report what remains broken, do not re-open what the evidence shows resolved), so an unremediated rerun can never suppress repeat findings. Ledger across all rounds: zero resurrections. The close completes only when a live backstop round returns non-blocking and its stamped row corroborates this close -- by construction, never by attestation.

reason.specifics: Adopting the routed ai-assignment.md recommendation for Session 3 (instruction surfaces, incident-3 reproduction UAT, the two releases): Codex CLI gpt-5.4 @ effort=medium -- high procedural precision for documentation updates, structured UAT checklist authoring, and dual-release mechanics across MD/JSON/TOML, the calibrated strength where less-capable models have previously failed on release mechanics; medium effort reflects the procedural (not novel-code) nature of the tasks.

residuals: evidence_sha256 binds the row to the exact filled prompt but cannot be recomputed at close (the working tree has moved on) -- the consumer-side check is presence + well-formedness; the recomputable half of the binding is the artifact hash (deliberate design, documented in verification_stamp.py).

The backstop lives in close_session.run only; the mark_session_complete python API path still refuses an unverified close via the tightened gate (floor holds by refusal, not by in-process verification) -- acceptable because every sanctioned close path goes through close_session.
A determined orchestrator forging the full stamped artifact set remains out of scope (spec residual 2) -- the stamp raises the floor from lazy shortcut to deliberate multi-artifact forgery.
